D3DPIXELSHADERDEF

The following structure defines the format used to define pixel shaders on Xbox. The structure consists of several DWORDs that closely represent the underlying hardware. This format replaces the TokenStream format defined by Direct3D on the PC, and it will be directly generated by the pixel shader assembler. Once a pixel shader has been created and set using the Direct3D functions IDirect3DDevice8::CreatePixelShader() and IDirect3DDevice8::SetPixelShader(), it can be modified on the fly. Each DWORD in the pixel shader format has a corresponding render state defined. These render states may be used to modify portions or all of the current pixel shader.

This structure holds all of the state information necessary to define an Xbox pixel shader. It is the structure generated when a pixel shader is assembled. Each member of this structure corresponds to a Direct3D render state that can be set at any time using the IDirect3DDevice8::SetRenderState() method.

The members of this structure have been ordered to enable the most efficient hardware load possible.

The values for each DWORD in the pixel shader structure may be set with macros defined in d3d8types.h.

typedef struct _D3DPixelShaderDef
{
   DWORD    PSAlphaInputs[8];          
   DWORD    PSFinalCombinerInputsABCD; 
   DWORD    PSFinalCombinerInputsEFG;  
   DWORD    PSConstant0[8];            
   DWORD    PSConstant1[8];            
   DWORD    PSAlphaOutputs[8];         
   DWORD    PSRGBInputs[8];            
   DWORD    PSCompareMode;             
   DWORD    PSFinalCombinerConstant0;  
   DWORD    PSFinalCombinerConstant1;  
   DWORD    PSRGBOutputs[8];           
   DWORD    PSCombinerCount;           
   DWORD    PSTextureModes;            
   DWORD    PSDotMapping;              
   DWORD    PSInputTexture;            
   DWORD    PSC0Mapping;               
   DWORD    PSC1Mapping;               
   DWORD    PSFinalCombinerConstants;  
} D3DPIXELSHADERDEF;

Members

PSAlphaInputs[8]
Alpha inputs for each stage. For more information, see PSRGBInputs[0-7] and PSAlphaInputs[0-7].
PSFinalCombinerInputsABCD
Final combiner inputs. For more information, see PSFinalCombinerInputsABCD.
PSFinalCombinerInputsEFG
Final combiner inputs (continued). For more information, see PSFinalCombinerInputsEFG.
PSConstant0[8]
C0 for each stage.
PSConstant1[8]
C1 for each stage.
PSAlphaOutputs[8]
Alpha output for each stage. For more information, see PSRGBOutputs[0-7] and PSAlphaOutputs[0-7].
PSRGBInputs[8]
RGB inputs for each stage. For more information, see PSRGBInputs[0-7] and PSAlphaInputs[0-7].
PSCompareMode
Compare modes for clipplane texture mode. For more information, see PSCompareMode.
PSFinalCombinerConstant0
C0 in final combiner.
PSFinalCombinerConstant1
C1 in final combiner.
PSRGBOutputs[8]
Stage 0 RGB outputs. For more information, see PSRGBOutputs[0-7] and PSAlphaOutputs[0-7].
PSCombinerCount
Active combiner count (Stages 0–7). For more information, see PSCombinerCount.
PSTextureModes
Texture addressing modes. For more information, see PSTextureModes.
PSDotMapping
Input mapping for dot product modes. For more information, PSDotMapping.
PSInputTexture
Texture source for some texture modes. For more information, see PSInputTexture.
PSC0Mapping
Mapping of c0 regs to Direct3D constants. For more information, see PSC0Mapping and PSC1Mapping.
PSC1Mapping
Mapping of c1 regs to Direct3D constants. For more information, see PSC0Mapping and PSC1Mapping.
PSFinalCombinerConstants
Final combiner constant mapping. For more information, see PSFinalCombinerConstants.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in D3d8types.h.

See Also

Directly Defined Pixel Shaders
